senses.text: so in NT, depon. pass., to be utterly at a loss, be in despair: absol. (as Ps, l.c.), II Co 4:8; τοῦ ζῆν, II Co 1:8.†
prop., a judicial sentence: II Co 1:9, R, mg.

an answer (v. Thayer, s.v.): II Co 1:9, R, txt. (In FlJ, Ant., xiv, 10, 6, of a rescript of the Senate; in Inscr. of an official decision, Deiss., BS, 257; a reply to a deputation, MM, s.v.: cf. also Cremer, 375).†

G5082.etymology: (altern. of τηλικόσδε, -ήδε, -όνδε, strength­ened form of τηλίκος), [in LXX: II Mac 12:3, III Mac 3:9, IV Mac 16:4 * ;]

senses.text: of persons,
of such an age, so old;
so young.
Of things, so great: II Co 1:10, He 2:3, Ja 3:4, Re 16:18.†
to help together, join in serving: c. dat. instr., II Co 1:11.†
sanctity, holiness, regarded, properly, as an abstract quality (v. next word, and cf. Lft. Notes, 49; MM, VGT, s.v.): II Co 1:12, He 12:10.†
confidence: II Co 1:15 3:4 10:2, Eph 3:12; seq. εἰς, II Co 8:22; ἐν, Phl 3:4 (the word is condemned by the Atticists, v. Rutherford, NPhr., 355).†
lightness, levity: II Co 1:17.†

G4610.etymology: (in MSS also Σιλβανός, a form freq. in π.), -οῦ, ὁ,

senses.text: Silvanus, latinized name of Silas: II Co 1:19, I Th 1:1, II Th 1:1, I Pe 5:12.†

an earnest, part payment in advance for security, a first instalment: II Co 1:22 5:5, Eph 1:14. (The word is found in cl. and was prob. brought to Greece by the Phœnicians (AR, Eph., 1:14). It is found in π. with both spellings (v. Milligan, NTD, 73). In MGr. ἀρραβῶνα is an engagement ring; v. MM, s.v.)†
to put a burden on, be burdensome: fig., absol., II Co 2:5; c. acc pers., I Th 2:9, II Th 3:8 (cf. M, Th., I, 2:9).†
citizenship, franchise.
As in Inscr. (LS, s.v.), LXX, l.c. (= cl. τὸ ἐπιτίμιον), punishment, penalty: II Co 2:6.†
by crasis for τὸ ἐνανίον, neut. acc, used adverbially, on the contrary, contrariwise: II Co 2:7, Ga 2:7, I Pe 3:9.†

G2964.etymology: (< κῦρος, 1. authority. 2. validity), [in LXX for קום;]

senses.text: to confirm, ratify, make valid: II Co 2:8; pass., Ga 3:15 (cf. προ-κυρόω).†

G4122.etymology: (< πλεονέκτης, q.v.), [in LXX: Hb 2:9, Ez 22:27 (בּצע), Jg 4:11 B*;]

senses.text: intrans., to have more, to have an advantage (cl., c. gen. pers.).
Trans., in late writers (v. M, Pr., 65), to overreach, defraud: c. acc. pers., II Co 7:2 12:17, 18; ἐν τ. πράγματι, I Th 4:6 (v. M, Th., in l.); pass., II Co 2:11 (as also in cl.; v. LS, s.v.).†
a thought, purpose, design: II Co 2:11 10:5 11:3, Phl 4:7. Meton., of the mind, II Co 3:14 4:4.†
Titus;
St. Paul's disciple and companion: II Co 2:13 7:6, 13, 14 8:6, 16, 23 12:18, Ga 2:1, 3, II Ti 4:10, Tit 1:4, subscr., Rec. (Τῖτος).
One surnamed Justus: Ac 18:7, RV (T, WH, Τίτιος).†

G2358.etymology: (< θρίαμβος, 1. a festal hymn to Bacchus. 2. The Roman triumphus),

senses.text: to triumph (and rarely, c. acc., to triumph over; so perh. Col 2:15, but v. infr.).
to lead in triumph: c. acc. pers., II Co 2:14; hence, generally, to make a spectacle or show of: Col 2:15 (but v. supr.; cf. MM, Exp., xv; and esp. Field, Notes, 181).†

G2175.etymology: (< εὐώδης, fragrant; < ὄζω) [in LXX for נִיחֹחַ, Ge 8:21, al. (ὀσμὴ εὐωδίας); Si 20:9 24:15, al. ;]

senses.text: fragrance: metaph., Χριστοῦ εὐ,., II Co 2:15; ὀσμὴ εὐωδίας (a metaphor of sacrifice, most freq. in Pent. and Ez.), Eph 5:2, Phl 4:18.†

G2585.etymology: (< κάπηλος, a huckster, cf. Is 1:22(LXX), Si 26:29),

senses.text: to make a trade of (RV, mg., make merchandise of), or perhaps (cf. Is 1:22(LXX)) to corrupt (RV, txt.): II Co 2:17.†
for putting together, constructive.
for bringing together, introductory, commendatory: ἐπιστολή (Arist., Diog. L., al.; and v. MM, xxiv), II Co 3:1.†
anything flat and broad.

subsenses.text: In late writers (Luc., al.), a flat stone, a tablet: II Co 3:3, He 9:4.†

senses.text: sufficiency, ability (Plat.): II Co 3:5.†
to make sufficient, render fit: c. dupl. acc., II Co 3:6; c. acc. pers. seq. εἰς, Col 1:12.†

G1795.etymology: (< τύπος), [in LXX for פִּתּוּחַ, Ex 36:39 (39:30)) A * ;]

senses.text: to imprint, engrave: pass. ptcp., c. dat., II Co 3:7.†
condemnation: II Co 3:9 7:3 (cf. Deiss., LAE, 91 f.).†
trans., to throw over or beyond.
Intrans., to run beyond. In both senses, metaph., to exceed, surpass, transcend: II Co 3:10 9:14, Eph 1:19 2:7; c. gen. obj., Eph 3:19.†

G2571.etymology: (< καλύπτω), [in LXX: Nu 3:25 4:8 ff. (מִכְסֶה), Ex 34:33 ff. (מַסְוֶה), etc.;]

senses.text: a covering, veil: II Co 3:13-16.†

G343.etymology: [in LXX chiefly for גָּלָה ni., pi.;]

senses.text: to unveil: metaph. of removing hindrance to perception of spiritual things, II Co 3:14 3:18.†
at which time, when; seq. ἂν, c. subjc., whensoever: II Co 3:15, 16.†
to show as in a mirror. Mid., to see oneself mirrored (v. MM, Exp., xv); c. acc. rei (R, txt., but v. mg.), to reflect as a mirror: II Co 3:18 (cf. Abbott, Essays, 94).†

G550.form: ἀπ-εῖπον, 2 aor. without present in use

senses.text: to tell out.
to forbid (III Ki 11:2).
to renounce: 1 aor. mid. (WH, App., 164; MM, VGT, s.v.), II Co 4:2.†
to ensnare.
As of wine, to adulterate, corrupt: τ. λόγον τ. θεοῦ, II Co 4:2.†
Trans. (cl.), to irradiate.
Intrans.

subsenses.text: (poët.), to see clearly (so perh. II Co 4:4; MM, s.v.);
as in LXX, to shine forth: II Co 4:4 (cf. δι-, κατ-αυγάζω).†

senses.text: illumination, light: metaph., I Co 4:4, 6.†

G3749.etymology: (< ὄστρακον, an earthen vessel or potsherd), [in LXX chiefly for חֶרֶשׂ;]

senses.text: made of clay, earthen: II Co 4:7, II Ti 2:20.†

G4729.etymology: (< στενός + χῶρος, space) [in LXX: Jos 17:15 (אוּץ), Jg 16:16 (אלץ pi.), Is 28:19 (20) (קצר) Is 49:19 (צרר), IV Mac 11:11 * ;]

senses.text: to be straitened (cf. Is 49:19); metaph., to be anxious (Hipp., al.).
In late writers, trans., to straiten, compress (LXX, Diod., al.; π. ap. MM, xxiii): pass., trop., II Co 4:8 6:12.†
to cast down, prostrate: metaph., pass., II Co 4:9.
to put down, lay down: metaph., mid., c. acc., θεμέλιον, He 6:1.†
to make new: II Co 4:16, Col 3:10 (v. Cremer, 323).†

G3910.etymology: (< πάραυτα = παρ’ αὐτά, sc. τὰ πράγματα), [in LXX: Ps 69 (70):3, To 4:14*;]

senses.text: immediately.
C. subst., to express brief duration (cf. ἡ π. λαμπρότης, Thuc., ii, 64), momentary, for a moment: II Co 4:17.†
= σκηνή,
a tent, tabernacle (C.I., 3071). Metaph., of the body as the tabernacle of the soul: II Co 5:1, 4.†

G3613.etymology: (< οἰκητήρ = οἰκήτωρ, an inhabitant), [in LXX: II Mac 11:2, III Mac 2:15*;]

senses.text: a habitation: Ju 6; trop., II Co 5:2.†
= -δύνω (Hdt.),
to put on over; pass. (Plut., al.), to have on over, be clothed upon: II Co 5:2, 4.†

G2292.etymology: (later form of θαρσέω), [in LXX: Pr 1:21, Ba 4:21, 27, Da LXX 6:16 (17), IV Mac 13:11 17:4*;]

senses.text: to be of good cheer or courage, to be confident: II Co 5:6, 8, He 13:6; τ. πεποιθήσει, II Co 10:2; seq. εἰς, II Co 10:1; ἐν, II Co 7:16.†

G2292.synonyms: SYN.: τολμάω. "θ. has reference more to the character, τ. to its manifestation" (Thayer, s.v. τολμάω).

senses.text: to live in a place, be at home: ἐν τ. σώματι, II Co 5:6 5:9; πρὸς τ. Κύριον, II Co 5:8.†

G1553.etymology: (< ἔκδημος, from home; cf. -ία, III Mac 4:11)*;

senses.text: to be from home, absent: II Co 5:6; seq. ἀπό, II Co 5:8; seq. ἐκ, II Co 5:9 (cf. ἀπο-, ἐνδημέω).†
to be the elder, to take precedence.
to be an ambassador (v.s. πρεσβύτης): II Co 5:20, Eph 6:20.†
to listen to.
to hearken to, hear with favour (one's prayer): c. gen. pers., II Co 6:2 (LXX).†
an occasion of stumbling, offence: II Co 6:3.†

G3469.etymology: (< μῶμος), poët. and late prose, [in LXX: Pr 9:7 (מוּם), Wi 10:14, Si 31 (34):18*;]

senses.text: to find fault with, blame: II Co 8:20; pass., II Co 6:3.†
sleeplessness, watching: II Co 6:5 11:27. (Plat., Hdt.; for exx. in π., v. MM, VGT, s.v.).†
purity, chastity (cf. ἁγιότης): II Co 6:6 11:3.†
intrans., to use evil words (Æsch.).
Trans., to speak ill of, defame (Soph.): pass., I Co 4:13.†
good report, praise: opp. to δυσφημία, II Co 6:8.†
to be unequally yoked: metaph., c. dat. pers., II Co 6:14.†

G3352.etymology: (< μετέχω), [in LXX: Ps 121 (122):3 א R (חבר pu.)*;]

senses.text: sharing, fellowship: II Co 6:14.†
concord, agreement: seq. πρός, c. acc. pers., II Co 6:15.†

G955.etymology: (another form of previous word, "due to harsh Syriac pronunciation," or else < Heb. בֵּל יַעַר, lord of the forest),

senses.text: Beliar, a name of Satan: II Co 6:15 (v. DB, i, 269).†
concord, agreement: II Co 6:16 (Polyb., Plut., al.).†

G1704.etymology: (Rec. ἐμπ-, v.s. ἐν) [in LXX: Le 26:12 Jb 1:7, al. (הָלַךְ hithp.), Wi 19:21 ;]

senses.text: to walk about in or among: seq. ἐν., dat. pers., II Co 6:16 (LXX),†
to admit, receive: II Co 6:17 (LXX) (Cremer, 687).†

G3841.etymology: (< πᾶς, κρατέω), [in LXX: freq. in Jb 5:8, 17, al. (שַׁדַּי), and for צְבָאוֹת, in the phrase θεός (κυρίος) π., II Ki 5:10, al., and freq. in Am, Za, Ma; also in Wi 7:25, Si 42:17 50:14, 17, and freq. in Jth, II, III Mac;]

G3436.etymology: (< μολύνω), [in LXX: Je 23:15 (חֲנֻפָה), I Es 8:83, II Mac 5:27*;]

senses.text: defilement: c. gen. obj., II Co 7:1 (Plut., FlJ).†
a fight.
a strife, contention, quarrel: II Co 7:5, II Ti 2:23, Ja 4:1; pl., Tit 3:9.†
longing: II Co 7:7, 11.†
indignation: II Co 7:11 (v. MM, VGT, s.v.).†

G53.etymology: (< ἅγος, v.s. ἅγιος), [in LXX chiefly for טָהוֹר (Pss, and Pr. only), also II Mac 13:8, IV Mac 18:7, al.;]

subsenses.text: free from ceremonial defilement , in a condition prepared for worship (for exx. of pagan usage, V. MM, VGT, s.v.).
As in OT (cl.), pure, chaste, undefiled, guiltless;
of persons: II Co 7:11 11:12, I Ti 5:22, Tit 2:5, I Jo 3:3;
of things: Phl 4:8, Ja 3:17, I Pe 3:2.†

G53.synonyms: SYN.: εἰλικρινής (q.v.), pure, primarily as winnowed, purged, first found in ethical sense in NT (sincere). On the equivalence of ἁ. and καθαρός (q.v.), v. DCG, ii, 459a though Westc. (Epp. Jo., 101) notes a distinction between them.

senses.text: beggary, destitution: II Co 8:2, 9, Re 2:9.†
self-chosen.
of one's own accord: II Co 8:3, 17.†
to begin before: II Co 8:6; c. acc., τὸ θέλειν, II Co 8:10 (not elsewhere).†

subsenses.text: prop., lawfully begotten, born in wedlock (in π., γυνὴ γ., a lawful wife; MM, s.v.).
true, genuine, sincere: Phl 4:3, I Ti 1:2, Tit 1:4; as subst., τὸ γ. = ἡ γνησιότης, sincerity, II Co 8:8.†

senses.text: to be a beggar, to beg (so chiefly in cl.).
to be poor as a beggar, to be destitute, poor: opp. to πλούσιος ὤν, II Co 8:9.†
last year, a year ago: ἀπὸ π. (as in π. v. Deiss., BS, 221; LAE, 70), II Co 8:10 9:2.†

G4295.etymology: [in LXX: Ex 39:18 (36), Nu 4:7 (פָּנִים), etc.;] used as pass. of προτίθημι;

senses.text: to be set before one, to be set forth: He 6:18 12:1, 2, Ju 7.
to present oneself, be present: II Co 8:12 (v. Mey., in l.).†
equality: II Co 8:13, 14.
equity, fairness: τὸ δίκαιον κ. τὴν ἰ., Col 4:1 (for distinction bet. τὸ δ. and ἡ ἰ., v. ICC, in l).†
to be less (RV, had no lack): II Co 8:15 (LXX) (a rare word; cf. MM, Exp., xii).†
in haste; hence, zealous, eager, diligent, earnest: seq. ἐν, II Co 8:22; compar., -οτεροσς ib. 17 (here in superl. sense, v. Bl., § 44, 3) 22.†
to send together with: c. acc. et dat., II Co 8:22; id. seq. μετά, c. gen., ib. 18.†
prop., to set, place, arrange, fit out; hence, mid., to set oneself for, prepare (Wi, II Mac, ll. c.).
to bring together, gather up (in Hom. of furling sails), hence to restrain, check; mid., to restrain or withdraw oneself, hold aloof, avoid: II Co 8:20; seq. ἀπό, II Th 3:6
(cf. ἀπο-, ἐξ-απο-, συν-απο-, δια-, ἐπι-, κατα-, συ(ν)-, ὑπο-στέλλω).†

G2042.etymology: [in LXX: Da LXX 11:10, 25 (גָּרָה hithp.), I Mac 15:40, II Mac 14:17, etc. ;]

senses.text: to stir up, provoke (as in cl.): Col 3:21.
In good sense (cf. ἐρεθισμός, excitement in MGr.), to stir up, stimulate: II Co 9:2.†
unprepared: II Co 9:4.†

G5287.etymology: (< ὑφίστημι, to set under, stand under, support) [in LXX for מַצָּב (I Ki 13:23 14:4 B), תִּקְוָה (Ru 1:12, Ez 19:5), etc., also in Wi 16:21 ;]

senses.text: a support, base or foundation (in various senses).
substance (Arist., al.; opp. to φαντασία, ἔμφασις): He 1:3
steadiness, firmness (Polyb., al.), hence, assurance, confidence: II Co 9:4 11:17, He 3:14 11:1 (here perhaps title-deed, as that which gives reality or guarantee; v. MM, xxv).†
to make ready beforehand: II Co 9:5.†
adv. from ptcp. (v. Bl., § 25, 1),
sparingly: II Co 9:6 (Plut.).†
to bring forth or forward. Most freq. in mid., to take by choice, prefer, propose: II Co 9:7.†

G2431.etymology: (< ἵλαος = ἵλεως, q.v.), [in LXX: Pr 19:12 (רָצוֹן) 22:8, Es 5:1, Jb 33:26, Si 13:26 26:4, III Mac 6:35*;]

senses.text: cheerful, joyous: II Co 9:7 (Pr 22:8(9)(LXX)).†
= δοτήρ,
a giver: II Co 9:7 (LXX) (not elsewhere).†
sufficiency (MM, s.v.): in subjective sense (v. Milligan, NTD, 57), II Co 9:8;
contentment: I Ti 6:6.†

G3993.etymology: (< πένομαι, to work for one's daily bread), [in LXX for אֶבְיוֹן, עָנִי, דַּל, etc.;]

senses.text: one who works for his living, a labourer, a poor man: II Co 9:9.†

G3993.synonyms: SYN.: πτωχός, properly a beggar and implying deeper poverty than π. (v. Tr., Syn., § xxxvi; Abbott, Essays, 78).

senses.text: to supply, provide: c. acc rei, II Pe 1:5; id. c. dat. pers., II Co 9:10, Ga 3:5; pass., Col 2:19, II Pe 1:11 (cf. χορηγέω, and v. MM, Exp., xiv).†

G5524.etymology: (< χορός, ἡγέομαι) [in LXX for כּוּל pilp. ;]

senses.text: to lead a χορός (v. LS, s.v.).
to defray the cost of a χορός.
In late writers, metaph., c. acc. (v. M, Pr., 65), to supply, furnish abundantly: II Co 9:10, I Pe 4:11 (Polyb., al.).†
to fill up by adding to, to supply fully: II Co 9:12 11:9.†
in cl., an agreement, assent, compact (in π., of a contract; Deiss., BS, 249).
confession (prob. always in an objective sense): II Co 9:13, I Ti 6:12, 13, He 3:1 4:14 10:13.†
inexpressible: II Co 9:15 (MM, VGT, s.v.).†

G4752.form: στρατεία (on the orthogr., v. Deiss., BS, 181 f.), -ας, ἡ

senses.text: an expedition, a campaign, warfare: metaph., II Co 10:4 (-τιά, T), I Ti 1:18.†
a pulling down, destruction: fig., κ. ὀχυρωμάτων, II Co 10:4; opp. to οἰκοδομή, II Co 10:8 13:10.†

G3794.etymology: (< ὀχυρόω, to fortify, make firm), [in LXX for מִבְצָר, etc.;]

senses.text: a stronghold, fortress (Ps 88 (89):40, Na 3:12, 14 al.); in LXX and NT, also metaph. of that in which confidence is placed (Pr 10:29 21:22, al.): II Co 10:4.†
to frighten away, terrify: c. acc pers., II Co 10:9.†
without measure: adverbially, εἰς τὰ ἄ., excessively, II Co 10:13 10:15†

G2583.etymology: (cf. κάννα, and Heb. קָנֶה, a reed), [in LXX: Mi 7:4, Jth 13:6, IV Mac 7:21 (and in Aq., Ps 18 (19):5, Jb 38:5)*;]

senses.text: a rod or bar (Jth 13:6).
a measuring rule; hence, metaph,
a rule or standard: Ga 6:16.
a limit (RV, province): II Co 10:13, 15, 16. (For the history of the word and esp. its later meanings, v. Westc., Canon, App. A; cf. also MM, Exp., xv; Cremer, 744.)†
to come to, to reach: seq. ἄχρι, II Co 10:13; εἰς, ib. 14.†
to stretch out overmuch: metaph., ἑαυτούς, II Co 10:14.†
comp. adv. (v. Bl., 28, 2; M, Pr., 99),
beyond: as prep. c. gen., τὰ ὑ. ὑμῶν, II Co 10:16 (Byz. and eccl.).†
to fit, join.
of marriage, to betroth. Mid.,

subsenses.text: to join to oneself, marry, take to wife;
to give in marriage: II Co 11:2 (for this there is no direct parallel. But v. M, Pr., 160; MM, s.v.).†

G2096.form: Εὔα (WH, Εὕα, § 408; Rec. Εὖα; S (in I Ti), Εὕα), -ας, ἡ (Heb. חַוָּה, Ge 3:20),

senses.text: Eve, wife of Adam: II Co 11:3, I Ti 2:13.†
c. acc. pers., to strip, plunder, spoil: II Co 11:8.†

G2655.etymology: (< ναρκάω, to grow numb, in LXX, in causal sense, for יקע, Ge 32:25(26); without Heb. equiv., Ge 32:32(33), Jb 33:19, Da LXX 11:6*);

senses.text: to cause to grow numb.
Intrans., to grow numb; hence metaph., to be inactive, be burdensome to c. gen., II Co 11:9 12:13, 14; pass., to be quite numb (Hippocr.).†
without weight; metaph. (MM, VGT, s.v.) not burdensome: II Co 11:9.†
a false apostle: II Co 11:13.†

G1386.etymology: (< δόλος), [in LXX—chiefly in Pss, Pr, Si—forמִרִמָהetc.;]

senses.text: deceitful: II Co 11:13.†
a wonder: II Co 11:14.
wonder: Re 17:6.†
to enslave: II Co 11:20, Ga 2:4.†
to be beside oneself, be deranged: II Co 11:23.†
above measure: II Co 11:23.†
five times: II Co 11:24.†
once: II Co 11:25, He 9:26, 27; ἔτι ἅ., He 12:26, 27; ἅ. τ. ἐνιαυτοῦ, He 9:7; καί ἅ. κ. δίς, twice: Phl 4:16, I Th 2:18;
once for all: He 6:4 9:28 10:2, I Pe 3:18, Ju 3, 5 (MM, VGT, s.v.).†
to suffer shipwreck: II Co 11:25; metaph., seq. περὶ τ. πίστιν, I Ti 1:19.†
lasting a night and a day; as subst., (τὸ) ν., a night and a day: II Co 11:25 (pl., Or. Sib., 8, 203).†

subsenses.text: the depth of the sea, the deep sea: II Co 11:25.†

senses.text: a false brother: of professing Christians, II Co 11:26, Ga 2:4.†

G3449.etymology: (= Hom. μόγος), in cl. chiefly poët., [in LXX for תְּלָאָה, עָמָל, etc.;]

senses.text: toil, labour, hardship, distress: II Co 11:27, I Th 2:9, II Th 3:8.†

G1373.etymology: (late form of δίψα), [in LXX chiefly for צָמֵא and cognates ;]

senses.text: thirst: II Co 11:27.†
an ethnarch, a provincial governor (cf. I Mac, ll. c.; FlJ, Ant., xiii, 6, 6; Dalman, 332): II Co 11:32.†
Aretas, an Arabian king: II Co 11:32 (Deiss., BS, 183 f., thinks the proper spelling Ἀρέθας was changed, as Schürer suggests, "by desire to Hellenise a barbaric name by assimilation to ἀρετή").†

G5432.etymology: (< φρουρός, a guard), [in LXX: I Es 4:56, Jth 3:6, Wi 17:16, I Mac 11:3 * ;]

senses.text: to guard, keep under guard, protect or keep by guarding: II Co 11:32; metaph., Ga 3:23, Phl 4:7, I Pe 1:5.†
of Damascus, Damascene: II Co 11:32.†
a plaited rope (Æsch.).
a hamper, a basket made of ropes: II Co 11:33.†

G731.etymology: (< ἀ- neg., ῥητός, ῥέω), [in Sm.: Le 18:23*;]

senses.text: unspoken (Hom., al.).
unspeakable (Hat., al.; freq. in Inscr.; MM, s.v.): II Co 12:4.†
to lift or raise over. Mid., to uplift oneself: II Co 12:7; seq. ἐπί, c. acc, pers., II Th 2:4 (cf. II Mac 5:23).†
anything pointed, esp.
in cl., a stake
In Hellenistic vernacular, a thorn (cf. LXX, ll. c.): σ. τῇ σαρκί, II Co 12:7 (cf. MM, i., xxiii; DB, iii, 700 f.; Deiss., St. Paul, 62 f.; Field, Notes, 187).†
to tent upon, spread a tabernacle over: metaph., seq. ἐπ’ ἐμέ (RV, rest upon, cover), II Co 12:9.†

G2274.etymology: (ἡσσ-, II Co 12:13, v.s. ἥσσων), [in LXX for חתת, etc.;] pass.,

senses.text: to be inferior: seq. ὑπέρ, II Co 12:13.
to be overcome: absol., II Pe 2:20; c. dat., II Pe 2:19.†
strengthened form of δαπανάω, to spend wholly; pass., with reflexive force, to spend oneself wholly: seq. ὑπέρ, II Co 12:15.†
ready to do anything;

subsenses.text: in cl., chiefly in bad sense, knavish, crafty: II Co 12:16;

senses.text: to send along with: c. acc., II Co 12:18 (cf. MM, xxiv).†
evil-speaking, railing: pl., II Co 12:20, I Pe 2:1.†
a whispering;
of secret slander, II Co 12:20 (Plut.);
of a murmured enchantment, Ec, l.c.†
a puffing up, swelling with pride: pl., II Co 12:20.†
to sin before: II Co 12:21 13:2.†
a strengthening, making fit: in ethical sense, II Co 13:9.†
abruptly, curtly, hence sharply, severely: II Co 13:10, Tit 1:13 (MM, s.v.).†
